summary: The Docker-based reproduction provisions a low-privilege user with only Media.elfinderConnection.read, authenticates through the bundled development gate and normal login flow, uploads a proof file through the elFinder connector, and confirms unauthorized file creation under public/media.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should prove that a user with only the read-scoped media connector permission can still create attacker-controlled file content in the public media filesystem.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the vulnerable repository at commit 418f0bf2b04ee9b046ea296c55d31466ecd0c919, built the reproduction image, bootstrapped CI4MS, logged in as mediareader@ci4ms.test, called /backend/media/elfinderConnection, uploaded evohunt-proof.txt, and printed the proof signal EVOHUNT_MEDIA_WRITE_PROOF before exiting successfully.
verification_method: The exploit helper logs in as the low-privilege user, opens the elFinder root, uploads a text/plain proof file, and then verifies the exact proof string. Verification first checks the returned media URL and, if that URL shape is not reliable, falls back to recursive on-disk confirmation under public/media so the oracle distinguishes a real unauthorized write from a generic login, startup, or parser success.
evidence_produced_by_run: The successful run printed [evohunt] SUCCESS EVOHUNT_MEDIA_WRITE_PROOF from the exploit helper.
The successful run printed [evohunt] verification succeeded: EVOHUNT_MEDIA_WRITE_PROOF and then [evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified.
The exploit helper confirmed that the uploaded proof file contents existed under public/media after the low-privilege connector upload.
Application logs generated during the run showed the low-privilege account issuing GET and POST requests to /backend/media/elfinderConnection.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
